metadata.title: Research ADCs: How T-DXd Unlocked HER2-Low Breast Cancer
metadata.color_semantics: teardown: INK #2A1A0E neutral/normal; CRIMSON #C8102E key finding/vulnerability; SLATE #545454 structure/neutral
metadata.note: CLI video REQUIRED spine: B00 intro · B01 PROBLEM · B02 ASK · B03 CODE · B04 OUTPUT(manim B04_ADCMechanism) · B05 CHANGE · B06 slate · B07 slate · B08 slate · B09 outro. Source: cancer-nanomedicine/chapters/05-antibody-drug-conjugates-as-nanoscale-medicines.md

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. The cleavable linker that redefined a patient population.

lines: The cleavable linker that redefined a patient population.

beats.narration_text: HER2-positive breast cancer was targetable from 1998. HER2-low breast cancer — fifty-five percent of all breast cancer — was not targetable, because the old ADC T-DM1 could not kill cells with low HER2 expression. Then T-DXd changed everything. The difference was the linker: cleavable versus non-cleavable. One enables bystander killing. One does not. The chemistry distinction created an entirely new patient population.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — title card: HER2-LOW: 55% OF BREAST CANCER, NOW TARGETABLE; contrast T-DM1 (non-cleavable, HER2-high only) vs T-DXd (cleavable, bystander killing)

beats.narration_text: In the terminal: ask Claude to research the mechanistic difference between T-DM1 and T-DXd — the DAR trade-off, cleavable versus non-cleavable linkers, and the DESTINY-Breast04 trial.

props.command:
claude "Research the mechanistic difference between T-DM1
and T-DXd that enabled HER2-low breast cancer treatment.
Explain: (1) the DAR trade-off (too low = insufficient
kill, too high = aggregation/clearance); (2) cleavable
vs. non-cleavable linkers and the bystander effect;
(3) what DESTINY-Breast04 showed (HER2-low as a new
targetable entity, PFS and OS benefit); and (4) why
lipophilicity of DXd payload matters for bystander
killing."

beats.role_note: terminal (Onda) — Python script reading Claude response
beats.narration_text: Read the code before trusting it. The script builds the ADC comparison table — T-DM1 versus T-DXd across linker type, bystander effect, HER2 threshold, and DESTINY trial result. Every row has a verified source. Read the table before accepting the claim.

props.code:
# adc_comparison.py — Claude Code output
ADCS = [
  {"name": "T-DM1",
   "linker": "non-cleavable (SMCC)",
   "bystander": "NO",
   "her2_min": "HER2-high (3+ or ISH+)",
   "trial": "EMILIA: OS 29.9 vs 25.9 mo"},
  {"name": "T-DXd",
   "linker": "cleavable (tetrapeptide)",
   "bystander": "YES — DXd diffuses to neighbors",
   "her2_min": "HER2-low (1+ or 2+/ISH-)",
   "trial": "DESTINY-B04 (NEJM 2022): PFS 9.9 vs 5.1 mo"},
]
for a in ADCS:
    print(f"{a['name']}: {a['linker']} | bystander={a['bystander']}")

beats.role_note: OPEN vox slot → Manim video B04_ADCMechanism
beats.narration_text: Run it. On the left, T-DM1: the antibody binds a HER2-high cell, internalizes, the payload is trapped in the lysosome — it kills only the cell it entered. HER2-low and HER2-negative neighbors are untouched. On the right, T-DXd: the cleavable linker releases the DXd payload, which diffuses out of the lysosome, out of the cell, and kills the neighbors. The green check marks on all three cell types on the right side are what DESTINY-Breast04 confirmed in patients.
beats.visual_intent: Manim B04_ADCMechanism: side-by-side — T-DM1 (left, non-cleavable, kills HER2-high only) vs T-DXd (right, cleavable, bystander killing of HER2-low and HER2-neg neighbors); DESTINY-Breast04 annotation at bottom

beats.narration_text: The bystander effect is not limited to HER2. Now iterate: ask Claude to survey two other ADCs where the linker-payload design enabled a new indication beyond the original target.

props.command:
claude "Survey the ADC field beyond HER2. Name two other
ADCs: (1) trastuzumab deruxtecan for HER2-low lung
cancer and (2) sacituzumab govitecan for TROP2-positive
TNBC. For each, explain what linker/payload design
enabled the new indication. What does the cleavable
linker + bystander mechanism generalize to?"

beats.narration_text: The pattern holds: sacituzumab govitecan uses a cleavable CL2A linker releasing SN-38, a potent topoisomerase inhibitor — bystander killing in TROP2-positive triple-negative breast cancer, approved 2020. T-DXd in HER2-low lung: the same cleavable linker as in breast, the same DXd payload, DESTINY-Lung02 showing response in a tumor type previously called untargetable for HER2.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — ADC expansion table: T-DXd HER2 breast | T-DXd HER2-low lung | sacituzumab TROP2 TNBC; each with linker type, bystander effect Y/N, and approval status

beats.narration_text: So the cleavable linker was not a small chemistry detail — it redefined which patients could benefit from an entire class of therapy. T-DM1 was designed to kill HER2-high cells. T-DXd was designed to kill in neighborhoods with heterogeneous HER2 expression. The linker choice changed the patient population by fifty-five percent.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — summary card: NON-CLEAVABLE LINKER → kills only expressing cells; CLEAVABLE LINKER → bystander effect → new patient populations; DESTINY-Breast04 as proof

beats.narration_text: Your move: check the DAR of any ADC you are studying — drug-to-antibody ratio below two and the kill is insufficient; above eight and aggregation drives clearance. Then check whether the linker is cleavable. If it is not, the bystander effect is zero regardless of payload potency.
beats.visual_intent: SLATE — next steps card: 1) check DAR (optimal range 3-8 for most ADCs); 2) verify linker cleavability; 3) check TROP2 expression in your tumor type for sacituzumab eligibility

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Build it with a CLI. Then take it apart. At Nik Bear Brown, nikbearbrown dot com.
